scenarios.opening_query: The dealership fired me for 'attitude' the same month I hit fifteen years of service.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'attitude' was me reporting odometer tampering on trade-ins to the state — my email to the DMV predates the write-ups by a week.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'attitude' was reporting odometer tampering to the state — my DMV email predates every write-up.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a fraud report preceded the discipline
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I never reported anyone — I refused to roll back an odometer myself, said so on the shop floor, and was gone in a month.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I refused to roll one back myself and said so on the floor — gone in a month.

scenarios.opening_query: My charter school non-renewed my contract after six years of 'exceeds expectations' reviews.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The non-renewals this year were me and the two other teachers over 55 — replaced by first-year hires at half our salaries, per the board's own budget memo.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The non-renewals were the three of us over 55, replaced by first-year hires at half the salary — it's in the board's own budget memo.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Ages varied — mine came a month after I told the principal I'd have to report the special-ed service shortfalls if they weren't fixed, and then refused to sign the compliance attestation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Mine came after I refused to sign the special-ed compliance attestation I knew was false.

scenarios.opening_query: I got 'laid off' from the clinic four days after HR learned what my medication is for.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The medication disclosed my HIV status — my duties never posed any risk, and the timing is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The medication disclosed my HIV status — my duties posed no risk, and the four-day timing tells the story.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a disclosed condition triggered the action
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The medication wasn't it — the 'layoff' came the week I told billing I wouldn't code visits at levels we didn't perform.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It wasn't the medication — it came the week I refused to upcode visits.

scenarios.opening_query: After my military spouse's PCS orders came through, my remote employer suddenly 'restructured' my role away.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > Discrimination - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our state protects military-family status — and my manager's email said the quiet part: 'can't build around someone who moves every two years.'
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Our state protects military-family status — and my manager wrote 'can't build around someone who moves every two years.'
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether protected military-family status motivated the cut
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The restructure came ten days after I flagged that our overtime tracker was shaving hours — and I'd refused to 'correct' my team's timesheets downward.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came after I refused to shave my team's timesheets.

scenarios.opening_query: Everyone tells me I have a discrimination case because I'm the only woman they've ever fired from the floor.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Discrimination - Employee || Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Honestly the firing followed my formal demand for two years of skipped rest-break pay — three men who complained about pay are gone too; it's wage retaliation, not sex discrimination.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The firing followed my written demand for two years of skipped break pay — three men who complained are gone too. It's wage retaliation.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which protected activity the pattern actually tracks
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My pay was fine — but the foreman's 'no women on nights' policy is in writing, and I was fired the week I bid a night shift.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Pay was fine — but 'no women on nights' is in writing, and I bid a night shift the week I was fired.
scenarios.vetting_note: Corrects the user's own theory via the comparator pattern.

scenarios.opening_query: My boss fired me by group text and is now telling our whole industry I 'stole clients.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The firing itself was lawful-if-ugly at-will — but the client-theft story is false, provable, and has cost me two job offers; the defamation is the real case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The firing was probably lawful at-will — but the client-theft story is false, provable, and has cost me two offers. Defamation's the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether post-termination falsehoods are the operative harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The story he tells is technically true — I did take my book of business, as my contract allows; the fight is the firing coming days before my vesting cliff.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: His story's technically true and my contract allows it — the real fight is being fired days before my vesting cliff.
scenarios.vetting_note: Falsity fact selects tort vs employment.
